seoTitle: Create Drip Email Campaigns in GoHighLevel: Guide
metaDescription: Build automated drip email sequences in GoHighLevel with triggers, delays, and personalization to nurture leads over time.
intro: Drip email campaigns let you nurture leads automatically over time instead of manually sending follow-up emails one by one. This tutorial covers building your email sequence in GoHighLevel, setting delays and triggers, personalizing messages, and connecting the campaign to your broader workflows.

steps.heading: Create a new drip campaign
steps.body: Start a new drip email campaign within GoHighLevel as the foundation for your automated sequence.

steps.heading: Build your email sequence
steps.body: Design the series of emails that will be sent to leads over time, each building on the last to nurture the relationship.

steps.heading: Configure triggers and delays
steps.body: Set the conditions that trigger each email and the delays between them so messages go out at the right time based on customer actions.

steps.heading: Personalize messages using contact data
steps.body: Use contact information to personalize emails, making the campaign feel more relevant to each recipient.

steps.heading: Test and launch your campaign
steps.body: Review the sequence, test it for accuracy, and launch the campaign so it begins nurturing leads automatically.

faq.question: What is a drip email campaign?
faq.answer: It's a series of automated emails sent over time to nurture leads and keep customers engaged based on triggers and delays you configure.

faq.question: Can I personalize emails within the drip sequence?
faq.answer: Yes, personalizing messages using contact data is one of the core steps covered in the tutorial.

faq.question: Can drip campaigns connect to other GoHighLevel tools?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers combining drip campaigns with workflows, CRM contacts, pipelines, tags, and marketing funnels.

faq.question: How do I control when each email is sent?
faq.answer: You configure triggers and delays between emails, which determines the timing of each message in the sequence.

faq.question: Who is this tutorial for?
faq.answer: Agencies, marketers, business owners, sales teams, email marketers, and GoHighLevel beginners.
